description:
You are a localization engineer who has just received an updated list of English source-strings.  
    Your job is to synchronise the existing French translation file with this list.

    The project directory already exists:

        /home/user/projects/l10n/

    and contains two files:

        1. /home/user/projects/l10n/en_US.csv  
           • Comma-separated, UTF-8, UNIX line-endings.  
           • First row is the header:  key,text  
           • Each subsequent row contains the message-identifier (column “key”) and the English source text
             (column “text”).  No embedded new-lines, no quotes; simple CSV.

        2. /home/user/projects/l10n/fr_FR.json  
           • UTF-8, pretty-printed, 2-space indentation, UNIX line-endings.  
           • Contains a JSON object whose property-names exactly match some of the keys that appear in
             en_US.csv.  
           • Any key that is missing from this JSON file still needs to be translated.

    Your tasks

    1. Parse /home/user/projects/l10n/en_US.csv and obtain every value that appears in the “key” column
       (ignore the “text” column completely).

    2. Open /home/user/projects/l10n/fr_FR.json.  
       For every key you found in step 1 that does NOT already exist as a
       property in fr_FR.json, add it with the literal JSON value "TODO"  
       (include the quotes, it must remain a JSON string).

    3. After inserting the missing keys, re-write /home/user/projects/l10n/fr_FR.json so that  
       • All properties are sorted in strictly lexicographical order (simple byte-order; e.g. "A" < "a").  
       • The file is pretty-printed with exactly two spaces per indentation level.  
       • There is a single trailing newline at the very end of the file.

    4. Produce a change-log called /home/user/projects/l10n/update.log with the following strict format:

          ADDED <key1>
          ADDED <key2>
          ...

       • Write one line for every key that was newly inserted into fr_FR.json.  
       • List the lines in the same lexicographical order you used in the JSON file.  
       • Each line must end with a UNIX newline (`\n`).  
       • Do NOT include keys that were already present in the JSON file before you started.

    Hints / constraints

    • Everything you need is already in /home/user/projects/l10n/.  
    • You do NOT need internet access or special permissions.  
    • Completing this assignment should take no more than 3–4 shell commands plus a tiny script  
      (you can choose Bash, Python, jq, awk, etc.).  
    • The automated test will fail if the JSON is not sorted, the indentation is wrong, or if the
      update.log deviates from the exact format described above.

    When you are done, the only files modified or created under /home/user/projects/l10n/ should be:

        • fr_FR.json   (updated, sorted, 2-space indent)  
        • update.log   (new, see exact format above)

    No other output is required.
truth:

    File: /home/user/projects/l10n/en_US.csv

    key,text
    app.title,Weather App

      "app.title": "Application Météo",
      "menu.settings": "Paramètres"
    }
    ----------------------------------------------------------------

    Expected state AFTER the agent finishes:

    File: /home/user/projects/l10n/fr_FR.json
    ----------------------------------------------------------------
    {
      "app.greeting": "TODO",
      "app.title": "Application Météo",
      "error.network": "TODO",
      "menu.quit": "TODO",
      "menu.settings": "Paramètres"
    }
    ----------------------------------------------------------------
    (exactly two spaces per indent, UNIX LF line endings, single trailing newline)

    File: /home/user/projects/l10n/update.log
    ----------------------------------------------------------------
    ADDED app.greeting
    ADDED error.network
    ADDED menu.quit
    ----------------------------------------------------------------
    (each line ends with LF, final newline present)

    Notes for the validator:
    • Byte-for-byte equality (including newline placement) will be checked for both files.
    • No additional files or directories should be present inside /home/user/projects/l10n/.
